mnemonics: CRAM: Clock Rate Affects Max-speed (for CPU speed being tied to clock rate)
SPEED: Sequential Paths Execute Every Delay (for critical path determining max clock speed)
FAST: Faster Access = Shorter Time (for memory access speed)
keyPoints: Clock speed (frequency) determines how many instructions per second a CPU can execute
Critical path is the longest delay path between registers - determines maximum clock speed
Memory hierarchy exists to bridge speed gap between fast CPU and slow memory
Cache hit rates directly impact effective memory access speed
Pipelining improves speed by allowing simultaneous execution of multiple instruction stages
commonMistakes: Assuming higher clock speed always means better performance (ignoring IPC)
Confusing latency with bandwidth in memory systems
Overlooking propagation delays in critical path calculations
Forgetting that cache misses drastically slow down memory access
Not considering clock skew in synchronous systems
